title: Define a Logical Printer
text: Define a Logical Printer On Windows, you can define a logical printer, which is a named collection of printer settings for a physical printer. You can define more than one logical printer for the same physical printer. The Uniface Setup utility must be enabled for your application. For more information, see Setup Utility . Output can be sent to the printer only if the device table specified in the print job model is appropriate to the platform and printer. For more information, see Define a Print Job Model . In the Uniface Setup utility, click the Printer tab and click Add . The Printer definition dialog is displayed, with the default physical printer for your machine automatically selected. Enter the name of the new logical printer, for example NETWORK . Click >> to display the Windows Print Setup dialog, and set the printer properties that you want this logical printer to have, such as orientation, paper size, paper source, double-sided, and so on, and press OK. Press OK in the Printer definition dialog. The new logical printer is now visible in the Printer tab. Click OK in the Printer tab to save the definition in the Registry and add the logical printer to the LogPrinter setting in usys.ini . Related Links LogPrinter Specifies one or more logical printers for the application. Windows only. Print Job Models A print job model is a named collection of printer attributes that determine how and where Uniface forms or reports are printed, including details of the device type, margins, and so on. A print job model must be available before you can print. Setup Utility The Setup Utility enables users to change selected initialization settings in usys.ini without restarting the application. It can be added to the system menu of an MDI Uniface desktop application. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
